summary: The Docker-based reproduction successfully demonstrates that the Bagisto customer downloadable products endpoint is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) with NO URL validation. The vulnerable copy() function in DownloadableProductController.php can be made to fetch internal-only resources when an attacker-controlled URL is stored in purchased downloadable products.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the application to fetch an internal-only HTTP endpoint (simulating cloud metadata or internal API) without any URL validation. The proof string 'EV0HUNT_AUTHENTICATED_SSRF_' should be present in the fetched content, demonstrating successful SSRF with no validation barriers.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully executed the vulnerable code path. The PHP copy() function fetched the mock internal metadata service at http://localhost/internal-metadata/ and returned sensitive data including simulated service account credentials, API keys, and internal endpoint URLs. The proof string 'EV0HUNT_AUTHENTICATED_SSRF_' was present in the response, confirming the SSRF vulnerability with no URL validation.
verification_method: The verify.sh script orchestrates the SSRF attack by: (1) Setting up a mock internal metadata service accessible only via localhost; (2) Running the vulnerable code that uses PHP's copy() function to fetch the internal URL without any validation; (3) Checking for the proof string 'EV0HUNT_AUTHENTICATED_SSRF_' in the response; (4) Verifying that sensitive simulated internal data was successfully fetched via SSRF with zero validation.
evidence_produced_by_run: The mock internal metadata service returned JSON containing proof string 'EV0HUNT_AUTHENTICATED_SSRF_'
The vulnerable copy() function successfully fetched the internal-only resource at http://localhost/internal-metadata/
The response contained simulated sensitive data including service account credentials, Stripe API key, SendGrid API key, and internal endpoint URLs
The proof file /tmp/ssrf_proof.txt was created with the full SSRF response
NO URL validation was performed - the code directly passed the database URL to copy() without any checks
This endpoint is MORE SEVERE than the sample endpoint because it has zero validation (not even the bypassable DNS check)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the local repository at advisory_1b594c8b/repo (no git clone needed for this PoC)
